- [Avalonia.Desktop Package](/api/packages/avalonia-desktop.md): Avalonia.Desktop Package
- [Avalonia.Fonts.Inter Package](/api/packages/avalonia-fonts-inter.md): Avalonia.Fonts.Inter Package
- [Avalonia.FreeDesktop Package](/api/packages/avalonia-freedesktop.md): Avalonia.FreeDesktop Package
- [Avalonia.Headless.NUnit Package](/api/packages/avalonia-headless-nunit.md): Avalonia.Headless.NUnit Package
- [Avalonia.Headless.Vnc Package](/api/packages/avalonia-headless-vnc.md): Avalonia.Headless.Vnc Package
- [Avalonia.Headless.XUnit Package](/api/packages/avalonia-headless-xunit.md): Avalonia.Headless.XUnit Package
- [Avalonia.LinuxFramebuffer Package](/api/packages/avalonia-linuxframebuffer.md): Avalonia.LinuxFramebuffer Package
- [Avalonia.Markup.Xaml.Loader Package](/api/packages/avalonia-markup-xaml-loader.md): Avalonia.Markup.Xaml.Loader Package
- [Avalonia.Native Package](/api/packages/avalonia-native.md): Avalonia.Native Package
- [Avalonia.Skia Package](/api/packages/avalonia-skia.md): Avalonia.Skia Package
- [Avalonia.Themes.Fluent Package](/api/packages/avalonia-themes-fluent.md): Avalonia.Themes.Fluent Package
- [Avalonia.Win32.Interoperability Package](/api/packages/avalonia-win32-interoperability.md): Avalonia.Win32.Interoperability Package
- [Avalonia.X11 Package](/api/packages/avalonia-x11.md): Avalonia.X11 Package
- [AvaloniaUI.DiagnosticsSupport Package](/api/packages/avaloniaui-diagnosticssupport.md): AvaloniaUI.DiagnosticsSupport Package
- [SourceGenerator Namespace](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator.md): SourceGenerator Namespace
- [OrgFreedesktopDBusProxy.OrgFreedesktopDBusProperties Class](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator/orgfreedesktopdbusproxy-orgfreedesktopdbusproperties.md): OrgFreedesktopDBusProxy.OrgFreedesktopDBusProperties Class
- [OrgFreedesktopIBusInputContextProxy.OrgFreedesktopIBusInputContextProperties Class](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator/orgfreedesktopibusinputcontextproxy-orgfreedesktopibusinputcontextproperties.md): OrgFreedesktopIBusInputContextProxy.OrgFreedesktopIBusInputContextProperties Class
- [OrgFreedesktopPortalFileChooserProxy.OrgFreedesktopPortalFileChooserProperties Class](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator/orgfreedesktopportalfilechooserproxy-orgfreedesktopportalfilechooserproperties.md): OrgFreedesktopPortalFileChooserProxy.OrgFreedesktopPortalFileChooserProperties Class
- [OrgFreedesktopPortalSettingsProxy.OrgFreedesktopPortalSettingsProperties Class](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator/orgfreedesktopportalsettingsproxy-orgfreedesktopportalsettingsproperties.md): OrgFreedesktopPortalSettingsProxy.OrgFreedesktopPortalSettingsProperties Class
- [OrgKdeStatusNotifierWatcherProxy.OrgKdeStatusNotifierWatcherProperties Class](/api/tmds/dbus/sourcegenerator/orgkdestatusnotifierwatcherproxy-orgkdestatusnotifierwatcherproperties.md): OrgKdeStatusNotifierWatcherProxy.OrgKdeStatusNotifierWatcherProperties Class
- [IL Namespace](/api/xamlx/il.md): IL Namespace
- [RecordingIlEmitter.LabelInfo Class](/api/xamlx/il/recordingilemitter-labelinfo.md): RecordingIlEmitter.LabelInfo Class
- [RecordingIlEmitter.LocalInfo Class](/api/xamlx/il/recordingilemitter-localinfo.md): RecordingIlEmitter.LocalInfo Class
- [RecordingIlEmitter.RecordedInstruction Class](/api/xamlx/il/recordingilemitter-recordedinstruction.md): RecordingIlEmitter.RecordedInstruction Class
- [Parsers Namespace](/api/xamlx/parsers.md): Parsers Namespace
- [CommaSeparatedParenthesesTreeParser.Node Class](/api/xamlx/parsers/commaseparatedparenthesestreeparser-node.md): CommaSeparatedParenthesesTreeParser.Node Class
- [CommaSeparatedParenthesesTreeParser.ParseException Class](/api/xamlx/parsers/commaseparatedparenthesestreeparser-parseexception.md): CommaSeparatedParenthesesTreeParser.ParseException Class
- [Transform Namespace](/api/xamlx/transform.md): Transform Namespace
- [NamespaceInfoHelper.NamespaceResolveResult Class](/api/xamlx/transform/namespaceinfohelper-namespaceresolveresult.md): NamespaceInfoHelper.NamespaceResolveResult Class
- [TransformerConfiguration.XamlValueConverter Delegate](/api/xamlx/transform/transformerconfiguration-xamlvalueconverter.md): TransformerConfiguration.XamlValueConverter Delegate
- [TypeSystem Namespace](/api/xamlx/typesystem.md): TypeSystem Namespace
- [XamlLocalsPool.PooledLocal Class](/api/xamlx/typesystem/xamllocalspool-pooledlocal.md): XamlLocalsPool.PooledLocal Class

## Controls

- [Controls](/controls.md): Avalonia comes with lots of high-quality UI controls, including buttons, lists, tabs and more, to quickly and easily build your app's user interface.
- [Carousel](/controls/data-display/collections/carousel.md): A reference for the Carousel control in Avalonia, which displays items one at a time with animated page transitions, navigation methods, and data-binding support.
- [ItemsControl](/controls/data-display/collections/itemscontrol.md): A reference for the ItemsControl in Avalonia, a base control for displaying repeating data with full control over layout and item appearance.
- [ItemsRepeater](/controls/data-display/collections/itemsrepeater.md): A virtualized, layout-driven control for displaying repeating data from a bound collection.
- [ListBox](/controls/data-display/collections/listbox.md): The ListBox displays items from an items source collection, on multiple lines, and allows individual or multiple selection.
- [ContentControl](/controls/data-display/contentcontrol.md): A base control that displays a single piece of content, which can be a string, a control, or a data-bound object rendered through a data template.
- [DataGrid](/controls/data-display/structured-data/datagrid.md): Deprecated
- [TreeDataGrid](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid.md): The TreeDataGrid combines tree view and data grid functionality in a single control, displaying hierarchical and tabular data together. It supports two modes:
- [Column types](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid/column-types.md): This control is available as part of Avalonia Accelerate Business or higher.
- [Expand and collapse operations](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid/expand-and-collapse.md): Learn how to programmatically expand and collapse rows in a hierarchical TreeDataGrid, respond to expand/collapse events, and implement lazy loading on demand.
- [Filtering](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid/filtering.md): Learn how to filter rows in the Avalonia TreeDataGrid control using predicate functions, including multi-criteria, enum, null-safe, and hierarchical filtering patterns.
- [Selection modes](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid/selection-modes.md): Two selection modes are supported:
- [Sorting](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treedatagrid/sorting.md): How to enable, disable, and customize column sorting in the Avalonia TreeDataGrid control.
- [TreeView](/controls/data-display/structured-data/treeview.md): The TreeView control can present hierarchical data and allows item selection. The items are templated so you can customise how they are displayed.
- [CodeHighlighter](/controls/data-display/text-display/codehighlighter.md): Adds syntax highlighting to code blocks rendered by the Markdown control, with ColorCode and TextMate implementations available as separate packages.
- [ImageLoader](/controls/data-display/text-display/imageloader.md): Customize how the Markdown control loads and resolves images by implementing a custom MarkdownImageLoader.
- [Label](/controls/data-display/text-display/label.md): A control that displays text and transfers focus to a target input element when clicked or activated by an access key.
- [Markdown](/controls/data-display/text-display/markdown.md): Render Markdown-formatted text using the Avalonia.Controls.Markdown control with support for theming, selection, and custom image loading.
- [Markdown styling](/controls/data-display/text-display/markdown-styling.md): The Markdown control in Avalonia supports custom styling via named resources. You can override these resources in your application to customize the appearance of Markdown elements.
- [SelectableTextBlock](/controls/data-display/text-display/selectabletextblock.md): A read-only text label that allows users to select and copy displayed text.
- [TextBlock](/controls/data-display/text-display/textblock.md): A read-only control for displaying formatted text, supporting multiple lines, inline formatting, and full font control.
- [TextTrimming](/controls/data-display/text-display/texttrimming.md): About the TextTrimming property
- [TransitioningContentControl](/controls/data-display/transitioningcontentcontrol.md): A content control that animates transitions when its content changes, supporting cross-fades, slides, and composite page transitions.
- [WindowNotificationManager](/controls/feedback/notification.md): A toast-style notification popup system that displays temporary messages at a configurable position within a window.
- [Popup](/controls/feedback/popup.md): The Popup is a low-level control that displays content in a floating window above other content. It serves as the foundation for higher-level controls like Flyout, ToolTip, ComboBox dropdowns, and context menus. Use Popup directly when you need custom positioning or dismissal behavior that those controls do not provide.
- [ProgressBar](/controls/feedback/progressbar.md): A horizontal or vertical bar that displays a value as a filled proportion, with optional text caption and indeterminate mode for unknown progress.
- [ToolTip](/controls/feedback/tooltip.md): The ToolTip is a popup that shows its content when the user hovers over the 'host' control to which it is attached.
- [Button](/controls/input/buttons/button.md): A clickable control that reacts to pointer input, raises a Click event, and optionally invokes an ICommand.
- [ButtonSpinner](/controls/input/buttons/buttonspinner.md): A content control with increment and decrement spinner buttons for cycling through values.
- [HyperlinkButton](/controls/input/buttons/hyperlinkbutton.md): A button styled as a text hyperlink that opens a URI using the platform's default handler.
- [RadioButton](/controls/input/buttons/radiobutton.md): A mutually exclusive option control that lets users select one choice from a group.
- [RepeatButton](/controls/input/buttons/repeatbutton.md): A button that repeatedly raises its click event while the user holds it down.
- [SplitButton](/controls/input/buttons/splitbutton.md): The SplitButton functions as a Button with primary and secondary parts that can each be pressed separately. The primary part behaves like normal Button and the secondary part opens a Flyout with additional actions.
- [ToggleButton](/controls/input/buttons/togglebutton.md): A button that toggles between checked and unchecked states, with optional three-state support.
- [ToggleSplitButton](/controls/input/buttons/togglesplitbutton.md): The ToggleSplitButton functions as a ToggleButton with primary and secondary parts that can each be pressed separately. The primary part behaves like a normal ToggleButton and the secondary part opens a Flyout with additional actions.
- [Calendar](/controls/input/date-and-time/calendar.md): The calendar is a control for users to select dates or date ranges.
- [CalendarDatePicker](/controls/input/date-and-time/calendardatepicker.md): A dropdown calendar control with a text box that lets users select or type a date.
- [DatePicker](/controls/input/date-and-time/datepicker.md): A spinner-based control that lets users select a date by picking year, month, and day values.
- [TimePicker](/controls/input/date-and-time/timepicker.md): A control that lets users select a time value using spinner controls for hours, minutes, and optionally seconds.
- [CheckBox](/controls/input/selectors/checkbox.md): A control that lets users toggle a Boolean value with a check mark, including optional three-state support for indeterminate values.
- [ColorPicker](/controls/input/selectors/colorpicker.md): The ColorPicker provides a highly customizable, general-purpose control that users can use to select colors in RGB or HSV color space. This implementation is just as much about providing primitive controls that developers can use to build their own color pickers as it is about providing a ready-to-use picker.
- [ColorView](/controls/input/selectors/colorview.md): Presents a color for user editing using a spectrum, palette and component sliders.
- [ComboBox](/controls/input/selectors/combobox.md): A drop-down selector that lets users pick a single item from a list, with optional editable text input and placeholder support.
- [NumericUpDown](/controls/input/selectors/numericupdown.md): A control that lets users enter and adjust a numeric value using spinner buttons, keyboard arrows, or the mouse wheel.
- [Slider](/controls/input/selectors/slider.md): A control that lets users select a numeric value by dragging a thumb along a track between minimum and maximum bounds.
- [ToggleSwitch](/controls/input/selectors/toggleswitch.md): A sliding on/off toggle control used for binary settings, with customizable on and off content.
- [AutoCompleteBox](/controls/input/text-input/autocompletebox.md): The AutoCompleteBox presents a text box for user input and a drop-down that contains possible matches from an items source collection, for the text typed in. The drop-down shows when the user starts to type, and the match is updated for each character typed. The user can select from the drop-down.
- [MaskedTextBox](/controls/input/text-input/maskedtextbox.md): The MaskedTextBox presents an area for typed (keyboard) input, but where the format and characters permitted can be constrained by a mask pattern formed from special characters.
- [TextBox](/controls/input/text-input/textbox.md): A single-line or multi-line text input control that supports watermarks, password masking, validation, and inner content.
- [VirtualKeyboard](/controls/input/text-input/virtualkeyboard.md): The VirtualKeyboard is a standalone control that provides an on-screen keyboard that can be manually placed in your application's layout. Unlike VirtualKeyboardScope, which automatically manages keyboard visibility based on focus, VirtualKeyboard is explicitly directed at a specific target input element.
- [Border](/controls/layout/containers/border.md): Learn how to use the Border control to add borders, backgrounds, rounded corners, and box shadows to your Avalonia UI content.
- [Expander](/controls/layout/containers/expander.md): A container control with an always-visible header and a collapsible content section that you can toggle open or closed.
- [Flyout](/controls/layout/containers/flyout.md): Flyouts are dismissible containers that can be attached to some classes of 'host' control; although flyouts themselves are not controls. They show when their host control receives the focus, and are hidden again in a number of different ways.
- [GroupBox](/controls/layout/containers/groupbox.md): A container control that visually groups related content under a header label with a surrounding border.
- [RefreshContainer](/controls/layout/containers/refreshcontainer.md): The RefreshContainer allows a user to pull down on content or a list of data to refresh the content or retrieve more data. The refresh progress is indicated by a RefreshVisualizer that appears from the edge by which the pull gesture was initiated. The content of a RefreshContainer must be a ScrollViewer, or a control that has one.
- [ScrollViewer](/controls/layout/containers/scrollviewer.md): A container control that provides scrollbars when its content exceeds the visible area.
- [SplitView](/controls/layout/containers/splitview.md): Learn how to use the SplitView control to create collapsible side panes and navigation sidebars in Avalonia UI.
- [Viewbox](/controls/layout/containers/viewbox.md): The Viewbox is a container control which can scale its contents. The way in which the contents are stretched can be defined, as well as when the stretch will occur (stretch direction).
- [VirtualKeyboardScope](/controls/layout/containers/virtualkeyboardscope.md): A container control that automatically shows and hides the virtual keyboard based on input focus.
- [Decorator](/controls/layout/decorator.md): A base class for controls that wrap and decorate a single child element, providing padding and a foundation for custom wrapper controls.
- [LayoutTransformControl](/controls/layout/layouttransformcontrol.md): A decorator control that applies layout-aware transforms such as rotation, scale, and skew to a single child, causing parent panels to measure and arrange around the transformed bounds.
- [Canvas](/controls/layout/panels/canvas.md): Learn how to use the Canvas panel in Avalonia to position child controls at absolute coordinates.
- [DockPanel](/controls/layout/panels/dockpanel.md): Learn how to use the DockPanel in Avalonia to dock child controls to the edges of a container.
- [Grid](/controls/layout/panels/grid.md): Learn how to use the Grid panel in Avalonia to arrange child controls in rows and columns.
- [GridSplitter](/controls/layout/panels/gridsplitter.md): A control that lets users resize columns or rows in a Grid by dragging a splitter bar at runtime.
- [Panel](/controls/layout/panels/panel.md): A basic layout control that overlays multiple child controls on top of each other, positioning them with alignment properties.
- [RelativePanel](/controls/layout/panels/relativepanel.md): Learn how to use the RelativePanel in Avalonia to position child controls relative to each other or the panel.
- [StackPanel](/controls/layout/panels/stackpanel.md): A panel that arranges child controls in a single line, either horizontally or vertically.
- [UniformGrid](/controls/layout/panels/uniformgrid.md): A panel that arranges its children in a grid where every cell is the same size.
- [WrapPanel](/controls/layout/panels/wrappanel.md): A panel that arranges child controls in a flowing, wrapping layout.
- [DrawingImage](/controls/media/drawingimage.md): A control that renders vector graphics as an IImage using Avalonia Drawing objects, enabling resolution-independent icons and shapes defined entirely in XAML.
- [Image](/controls/media/image.md): The image can display raster images from a specified image source. The source can be:
- [Implementing media playback with MediaPlayer](/controls/media/media-playback.md): This guide provides a practical introduction to implementing media playback in Avalonia applications using the Avalonia Accelerate MediaPlayer.
- [MediaPlayer](/controls/media/mediaplayer.md): The MediaPlayer class provides the core functionality for media playback in Avalonia applications. It handles media
- [MediaPlayerControl](/controls/media/mediaplayercontrol.md): The MediaPlayerControl is a fully-featured UI control for media playback that provides transport controls, progress display, volume control, and video rendering. It encapsulates a MediaPlayer instance and provides a rich user interface for media playback.
- [MediaSource](/controls/media/mediasource.md): The MediaSource class hierarchy provides an abstraction for different types of media content sources in Avalonia Accelerate MediaControls. This allows the media playback system to handle various content sources (files, URLs, streams) through a unified interface.
- [PathIcon](/controls/media/pathicon.md): A control that renders a vector icon from SVG path (stream geometry) data, commonly used inside buttons, menus, and other interactive controls.
- [ContextMenu](/controls/menus/contextmenu.md): A popup menu that appears when the user right-clicks a control, providing contextual actions.
- [Menu](/controls/menus/menu.md): The menu control can add menu structure to an application. You will usually place a menu at the top edge of a dock panel control, so that it is drawn at the top of a window.
- [MenuFlyout](/controls/menus/menuflyout.md): A flyout that displays a simple menu of commands, typically attached to a button or other control.
- [NativeMenu](/controls/menus/nativemenu.md): The NativeMenu can display a menu on macOS and some Linux distributions. It can be used in several contexts:
- [Separator](/controls/menus/separator.md): A visual divider used inside Menu, ContextMenu, and MenuFlyout controls to group related menu items.
- [CommandBar](/controls/navigation/commandbar.md): A CommandBar is a toolbar-style control that displays a row of primary commands and an overflow menu for secondary commands. It is commonly used to surface the most relevant actions for the current context, while keeping less frequently used commands accessible through the overflow ("more") button.
- [ContentPage](/controls/navigation/contentpage.md): ContentPage is the foundational building block for screen-based UI in Avalonia. It represents a single page of content and provides built-in support for headers, icons, safe area padding, and command bars. Every screen the user sees is typically a ContentPage (or a subclass of one).
- [DrawerPage](/controls/navigation/drawerpage.md): The DrawerPage combines a sliding drawer pane with a main content area, providing a common navigation pattern for applications. It is built on top of SplitView and adds page-based navigation features such as lifecycle events, safe area support, and automatic integration with NavigationPage.
- [NavigationPage](/controls/navigation/navigationpage.md): The NavigationPage manages a stack-based navigation system, allowing you to push and pop pages with animated transitions. It displays a navigation bar with a back button and the current page's header.
- [TabbedPage](/controls/navigation/tabbedpage.md): The TabbedPage displays a collection of pages through a tab strip, allowing users to switch between pages by selecting tabs. Each child page becomes a tab, with the tab header and icon derived from the page's Header and Icon properties.
- [TabControl](/controls/navigation/tabcontrol.md): A guide to the Avalonia TabControl, which organizes content into switchable tabbed pages.
- [TabStrip](/controls/navigation/tabstrip.md): A tab header strip without built-in content switching, giving you full control over how selected tab content is displayed.
- [TrayIcon](/controls/navigation/trayicon.md): A system tray icon control that displays an icon and native context menu in the operating system notification area.
- [ScrollBar](/controls/primitives/scrollbar.md): A primitive control that provides a draggable thumb and track for scrolling content in a horizontal or vertical direction.
- [ThemeVariantScope](/controls/primitives/themevariantscope.md): A primitive control that overrides the active theme variant (light or dark) for a section of the visual tree.
- [UserControl](/controls/primitives/usercontrol.md): A base class for creating reusable composite controls with a predefined XAML layout.
- [Window](/controls/primitives/window.md): A top-level content control that represents an operating-system window with title bar, icon, and close/minimize/maximize chrome.
- [WindowDrawnDecorations](/controls/primitives/windowdrawndecorations.md): A logical element that manages the client-side presentation of window decorations, such as the titlebar and frame. It also defines interactions with caption buttons.
- [Tags](/controls/tags.md)
- [20 docs tagged with "accelerate"](/controls/tags/accelerate.md)
- [NativeWebDialog](/controls/web/nativewebdialog.md): NativeWebDialog is a dialog window that hosts a native web browser. It is useful on platforms like Linux where an embedded NativeWebView control is not available, or when you want to show web content in a separate window without embedding it in your layout.
- [NativeWebView](/controls/web/nativewebview.md): This control is available as part of Avalonia Accelerate Business or higher.
- [WebAuthenticationBroker](/controls/web/webauthenticationbroker.md): This control is available as part of Avalonia Accelerate Business or higher.
- [WebView environment](/controls/web/webview-environment.md): This control is available as part of Avalonia Accelerate Business or higher.

## Docs

- [Accessibility](/docs/app-development/accessibility.md): Implement accessible Avalonia apps with AutomationProperties, keyboard navigation, and custom peers.
- [Setting up a cross-platform solution](/docs/app-development/cross-platform-solution-setup.md): Structure an Avalonia solution with a shared core project and platform-specific project heads.
- [Data validation](/docs/app-development/data-validation.md): Validate user input in Avalonia using DataAnnotations, INotifyDataErrorInfo, and exception-based validation.
- [Implementing dependency injection](/docs/app-development/dependency-injection.md): Set up dependency injection in an Avalonia app using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.
- [Embedding web content](/docs/app-development/embedding-web-content.md): Embed web content in Avalonia apps using NativeWebView, NativeWebDialog, and WebAuthenticationBroker.
- [Localizing using ResX](/docs/app-development/localizing.md): Localize Avalonia apps with ResX resource files, runtime language switching, and RTL support.
- [Logging errors and warnings](/docs/app-development/logging-errors-and-warnings.md): Enable and configure Avalonia diagnostic logging using the LogToTrace method and log areas.
- [Native platform interop](/docs/app-development/native-interop.md): Access native platform APIs, embed native views, and use P/Invoke in Avalonia applications.
- [Performance optimization](/docs/app-development/performance.md): Optimize Avalonia app performance with virtualization, layout efficiency, compiled bindings, and profiling.
- [Creating a resource dictionary](/docs/app-development/resource-dictionary.md): Create, include, and merge resource dictionary files to organize reusable XAML resources.
- [Resources overview](/docs/app-development/resources.md): Define, reference, and manage reusable XAML resources, merged dictionaries, and theme variants.
- [Handling unhandled exceptions](/docs/app-development/setting-unhandled-exceptions.md): Handle unhandled exceptions in Avalonia apps from the UI thread, background threads, and tasks.
- [Threading model](/docs/app-development/threading.md): Learn how Avalonia's single-threaded UI model works with dispatchers, async patterns, and background tasks.
- [Window management](/docs/app-development/window-management.md): Create, configure, and manage windows and dialogs in Avalonia desktop applications.
- [XAML live preview and design-time settings](/docs/app-development/xaml-preview-and-design-settings.md): How to use a live XAML previewer when developing with Avalonia. How to set design-time properties and data to simplify UI development.
- [Breaking changes in Avalonia 12](/docs/avalonia12-breaking-changes.md): A complete list of breaking changes between Avalonia 11 and 12, with migration guidance and code examples for each change.
- [Creating custom controls](/docs/custom-controls.md): Overview of approaches for building custom controls in Avalonia, from user controls to templated controls.
- [How to create attached properties](/docs/custom-controls/attached-properties.md): Define attached properties that can be set on other controls, like layout positioning properties.
- [Choosing a custom control type](/docs/custom-controls/choosing-a-custom-control-type.md): Compare user controls, templated controls, and custom-drawn controls to pick the right approach.
- [Control trees](/docs/custom-controls/control-trees.md): Understand how Avalonia organizes controls into logical and visual trees for layout and rendering.
- [Add a custom control class](/docs/custom-controls/custom-control-class.md): Create a new custom control class file in an Avalonia project using IDE tooling or manually.
- [How to Create and Reference a Custom Control Library](/docs/custom-controls/custom-control-library.md): This guide shows you how to create a custom control library and reference it for use in an Avalonia UI app.
- [How to create a custom flyout](/docs/custom-controls/custom-flyout.md): Create a custom flyout control by extending PopupFlyoutBase in Avalonia.
- [Custom ItemsPanel](/docs/custom-controls/custom-itemspanel.md): Replace the default items panel in an ItemsControl with a custom panel like Canvas.
- [Creating a custom panel](/docs/custom-controls/custom-panel.md): Implement a custom layout panel by overriding MeasureOverride and ArrangeOverride.
- [Defining events](/docs/custom-controls/defining-events.md): Define and raise routed events on custom Avalonia controls with tunneling and bubbling strategies.
- [Defining properties](/docs/custom-controls/defining-properties.md): Define styled, direct, and attached properties on custom Avalonia controls.
- [Drawing custom controls](/docs/custom-controls/drawing-custom-controls.md): Render custom visuals by overriding the Render method and using DrawingContext operations.
- [How to create templated controls](/docs/custom-controls/templated-controls.md): Build lookless templated controls with control themes, template parts, and pseudo-classes.
- [How to bind style classes](/docs/data-binding/binding-classes.md): Bind style classes to boolean properties to conditionally apply styling to Avalonia controls.
- [Binding debugging](/docs/data-binding/binding-debugging.md): Debug data binding errors in Avalonia using trace-level logging and diagnostic output.
- [How to bind from code](/docs/data-binding/binding-from-code.md): Create and manage data bindings from C# code rather than XAML.
- [Binding to commands](/docs/data-binding/binding-to-commands.md): Bind UI controls to ICommand implementations for handling user actions in the MVVM pattern.
- [How to bind to a control](/docs/data-binding/binding-to-controls.md): Bind one control's property to another control's property using ElementName or source bindings.
- [Validation in data binding](/docs/data-binding/binding-validation.md): Validate bound data using DataAnnotations, INotifyDataErrorInfo, or exception-based approaches.
- [Built-in data binding converters](/docs/data-binding/built-in-data-binding-converters.md): Reference of built-in value converters for common data binding transformations in Avalonia.
- [Sorting, filtering, and grouping collections](/docs/data-binding/collection-views.md): Sort, filter, and group bound collections using DataGridCollectionView and DynamicData.
- [Compiled bindings](/docs/data-binding/compiled-bindings.md): Use compiled bindings for compile-time validation and improved performance in Avalonia XAML.
- [Data binding syntax](/docs/data-binding/data-binding-syntax.md): Reference for Avalonia data binding markup syntax including paths, modes, converters, and fallback values.
- [Data context](/docs/data-binding/data-context.md): Understand how DataContext provides the default data source for bindings and inherits through the control tree.
- [How to bind can execute](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-can-execute.md): Enable and disable buttons automatically by binding to the CanExecute method of a command.
- [How to bind image files](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-image-files.md): Bind image sources from file paths, resources, or streams to display images in Avalonia controls.
- [How to bind multiple properties](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-multiple-properties.md): Bind multiple view model properties to controls and combine them with multi-value converters.
- [How to bind tabs](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-tabs.md): Bind a TabControl to a collection of view models to create dynamic tabbed interfaces.
- [How to bind to a collection](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-to-a-collection.md): Bind an ObservableCollection to list controls so the UI updates automatically when items are added, removed, or modified.
- [How to bind to a task result](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-to-a-task-result.md): Bind control properties to the result of an async Task to display data when it completes.
- [How to bind to an observable](/docs/data-binding/how-to-bind-to-an-observable.md): Bind control properties to IObservable streams for reactive data updates in the UI.
- [How to create a custom data binding converter](/docs/data-binding/how-to-create-a-custom-data-binding-converter.md): Implement IValueConverter to transform data between source and target in Avalonia bindings.
- [How to use INotifyPropertyChanged](/docs/data-binding/inotifypropertychanged.md): Implement INotifyPropertyChanged to notify the UI when view model properties change.
- [Introduction to data binding](/docs/data-binding/introduction-to-data-binding.md): Learn how Avalonia data binding connects UI controls to data sources using XAML markup extensions.
- [Markup extensions](/docs/data-binding/markup-extensions.md): Create custom XAML markup extensions that provide values to properties at runtime.
- [Master-detail binding](/docs/data-binding/master-detail.md): Implement master-detail patterns where selecting an item displays its details in a bound view.
- [MultiBinding](/docs/data-binding/multi-binding.md): Combine multiple binding sources into a single value using MultiBinding and IMultiValueConverter.
- [Content templates](/docs/data-templates/content-templates.md): Use the ContentTemplate property to define how a control displays data objects.
- [Control content](/docs/data-templates/control-content.md): Understand how controls display non-control content and why data templates are needed.
- [Creating data templates in code](/docs/data-templates/creating-data-templates-in-code.md): Create data templates in C# using FuncDataTemplate or by implementing IDataTemplate.
- [Data template collection](/docs/data-templates/data-template-collection.md): Define multiple data templates in a control's DataTemplates collection to match by type.
- [Introduction to data templates](/docs/data-templates/introduction-to-data-templates.md): Define how Avalonia displays data objects using data templates with type matching and reuse.
- [Reusing data templates](/docs/data-templates/reusing-data-templates.md): Share data templates across windows by defining them in the Application.DataTemplates collection.
- [View locator](/docs/data-templates/view-locator.md): Automatically resolve views for view models using a ViewLocator that implements IDataTemplate.
- [Android](/docs/deployment/android.md): Build, sign, and publish an Avalonia application as an APK or AAB for Android devices.
- [Embedded Linux](/docs/deployment/embedded-linux.md): Publish, transfer, and run an Avalonia application on an embedded Linux device using DRM/KMS.
- [iOS](/docs/deployment/ios.md): Build, sign, and distribute an Avalonia application for iPhone, iPad, and Mac Catalyst.
- [Desktop Linux](/docs/deployment/linux.md): Avalonia Linux programs can be executed in most Linux distros by double-clicking the executable or by starting it from the terminal. Nevertheless, for a better user experience, it is recommended to have the program installed, so the user can start it via desktop shortcut, that exists in desktop environments such as GNOME and KDE, or via command-line, by having the program added to PATH.
- [macOS](/docs/deployment/macos.md): macOS applications are distributed as .app bundles. Despite the .app extension, a bundle is actually a directory with a defined structure that macOS treats as a single launchable application. It contains your compiled executable, resources such as icons and images, and metadata that tells macOS how to run and display your app. To make .NET and Avalonia projects work in a .app bundle, some extra work is required after publishing.
- [Native AOT](/docs/deployment/native-aot.md): Publish Avalonia applications as native executables using ahead-of-time compilation.
- [WebAssembly](/docs/deployment/webassembly.md): When you publish an Avalonia Browser project, the .NET SDK compiles your application into WebAssembly (Wasm), a binary format that runs directly in the browser. The output is a static site containing an index.html file, your compiled .wasm modules, .NET runtime files, and any static assets. There is no server-side code. Everything executes client-side in the browser's Wasm runtime.
- [Events overview](/docs/events.md): Understand how routed events travel through the element tree in Avalonia.
- [Input events](/docs/events/input-events.md): Handle pointer, keyboard, and gesture input events in Avalonia controls.
- [Lifecycle events](/docs/events/lifecycle-events.md): Control initialization, visual tree attachment, and teardown events in Avalonia.
- [Application lifetimes](/docs/fundamentals/application-lifetimes.md): Choose and configure application lifetime models for desktop, mobile, and browser platforms.
- [Avalonia architecture](/docs/fundamentals/architecture.md): How controls are measured, arranged, rendered, and connected to platform backends.
- [Assets](/docs/fundamentals/assets.md): Include and reference bitmap, style, and resource dictionary assets in Avalonia applications.
- [Avalonia XAML](/docs/fundamentals/avalonia-xaml.md): Learn the XAML markup language used to define Avalonia user interfaces.
- [Code-behind](/docs/fundamentals/code-behind.md): Use code-behind files to access controls, set properties, and handle events from XAML.
- [Code-only UI](/docs/fundamentals/coded-ui.md): Build Avalonia applications entirely in C# or F# without XAML files.
- [Cross-platform architecture](/docs/fundamentals/cross-platform-architecture.md): Understand how Avalonia shares code across platforms and handles platform differences.
- [Main window](/docs/fundamentals/main-window.md): Set and access the main window or main view for desktop, mobile, and browser platforms.
- [The MVVM pattern](/docs/fundamentals/the-mvvm-pattern.md): Separate UI from logic using the Model-View-ViewModel pattern with data binding.
- [Top level](/docs/fundamentals/top-level.md): Access windowing, clipboard, storage, and other services through the TopLevel base class.
- [UI composition](/docs/fundamentals/ui-composition.md): Compose layouts from windows, built-in controls, user controls, and custom controls.
- [Visual and logical trees](/docs/fundamentals/visual-and-logical-trees.md): Understand the logical and visual tree structures used for layout, rendering, and events.
- [Getting started](/docs/get-started.md): Get started with Avalonia, the cross-platform .NET UI framework. Install the SDK, set up your IDE, and create your first project.
- [Create your first project](/docs/get-started/create-your-first-project.md): Create and run your first Avalonia application using the MVVM template in your preferred IDE or from the command line.
- [Install Avalonia](/docs/get-started/install-avalonia.md): Install the .NET SDK and Avalonia project templates so you can create Avalonia applications from the command line or your IDE.
- [Set up your IDE](/docs/get-started/set-up-your-ide.md): Configure your preferred IDE for Avalonia development. Install extensions for Visual Studio, Visual Studio Code, or JetBrains Rider.
- [Starter tutorial](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial.md): Learn the basics of Avalonia by building a temperature converter app. Part 1 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Adding a control](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/adding-a-control.md): How to add a button to your temperature converter app. Part 2 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Adding some layout](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/adding-some-layout.md): How to use layout controls to have multiple controls on your temperature converter app. Part 3 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Converting data](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/converting-data.md): How to use code-behind to execute a response when the button is clicked. Part 6 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Customizing the Avalonia window](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/customizing-the-avalonia-window.md): How to adjust the attributes of the Avalonia window. Part 4 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Establishing events and responses](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/establishing-events-and-responses.md): How to create an event handler to detect when the button is being clicked. Part 5 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Exercises](/docs/get-started/starter-tutorial/exercises.md): Test your knowledge of Avalonia on three coding exercises. Part 7 of 7 the Avalonia starter tutorial.
- [Adding icons](/docs/graphics-animation/adding-icons.md): Add icons to your Avalonia application using image files, icon fonts, or path icons.
- [Animation settings](/docs/graphics-animation/animation-settings.md): Configuration options for keyframe animations including easing, fill mode, and playback.
- [Animations](/docs/graphics-animation/animations.md): Overview of animation types in Avalonia including keyframe, transitions, and composition.
- [Bitmap blend modes](/docs/graphics-animation/bitmap-blend-modes.md): Bitmap blend modes for controlling how pixels combine during rendering in Avalonia.
- [Brushes](/docs/graphics-animation/brushes.md): Brush types for painting surfaces in Avalonia including solid colors, gradients, and tile brushes.
- [Clipping and masking](/docs/graphics-animation/clipping-and-masking.md): Clipping and opacity masking techniques for restricting visible content in Avalonia.
- [Composition animations](/docs/graphics-animation/composition-animations.md): Code-driven render-thread animations using the composition animation API in Avalonia.
- [Setting control transitions](/docs/graphics-animation/control-transitions.md): Configure transitions that animate property changes on Avalonia controls.
- [Custom rendering](/docs/graphics-animation/custom-rendering.md): Draw custom graphics by overriding the Render method with DrawingContext.
- [Drawing graphics](/docs/graphics-animation/drawing-graphics.md): Overview of the Avalonia 2D graphics system including shapes, geometries, and animations.
- [Easing functions](/docs/graphics-animation/easing-functions.md): Available easing functions for controlling animation timing curves in Avalonia.
- [Effects](/docs/graphics-animation/effects.md): Visual effects including box shadows, clipping, and opacity masks for Avalonia controls.
- [Gradients](/docs/graphics-animation/gradients.md): How to use LinearGradientBrush, RadialGradientBrush, and ConicGradientBrush to create color transitions in Avalonia UI.
- [Hit testing](/docs/graphics-animation/hit-testing.md): How Avalonia determines which visual element is at a given screen coordinate.
- [Image interpolation](/docs/graphics-animation/image-interpolation.md): Control image interpolation quality when scaling images in Avalonia.
- [Using keyframe animations](/docs/graphics-animation/keyframe-animations.md): Define keyframe animations in XAML to animate control properties over a timeline.
- [Setting page transitions](/docs/graphics-animation/page-transitions.md): Configure page transitions for animating between views in Avalonia using CrossFade, PageSlide, CompositePageTransition, or your own custom transitions.
- [Render transforms vs layout transforms](/docs/graphics-animation/render-vs-layout-transforms.md): Differences between render transforms and layout transforms in Avalonia.
- [Shapes and geometries](/docs/graphics-animation/shapes-and-geometries.md): Shape controls and geometry types for drawing 2D vector graphics in Avalonia.
- [Text options](/docs/graphics-animation/text-options.md): TextOptions attached properties for controlling text hinting, alignment, and rendering mode.
- [Transforms](/docs/graphics-animation/transforms.md): Render transforms and layout transforms for modifying position, size, rotation, and skew.
- [How to: Use the Clipboard](/docs/how-to/clipboard-how-to.md): Copy and paste text, images, and custom data using the Avalonia clipboard API.
- [How to: Build a Complete App Without XAML](/docs/how-to/coded-ui-how-to.md): Build a fully functional Avalonia application using only C# with no XAML files.
- [How to: Work with ComboBox](/docs/how-to/combobox-how-to.md): Bind collections, create custom templates, use editable combo boxes, and bind enums with the Avalonia ComboBox control.
- [How to add a custom font](/docs/how-to/custom-font-how-to.md): Add a custom font to an Avalonia application as a static resource or embedded font collection.
- [How to: Save and Load Application Settings](/docs/how-to/data-persistence-how-to.md): Persist user preferences and application state across sessions using JSON files or databases.
- [How to: Work with DataGrid](/docs/how-to/datagrid-how-to.md): Sorting, filtering, grouping, template columns, selection, validation, and editing with DataGrid.
- [How to: Work with Date and Time Pickers](/docs/how-to/datepicker-how-to.md): Bind dates, format display, validate input, and configure date ranges with Avalonia pickers.
- [How to: Debug Common Avalonia Issues](/docs/how-to/debugging-how-to.md): Debug bindings, layout, styling, and rendering issues in Avalonia applications.
- [How to: Work with Dialogs](/docs/how-to/dialogs-how-to.md): Create and show modal dialogs, return results, and build custom dialog windows in Avalonia.
- [How to: Implement Drag and Drop](/docs/how-to/drag-and-drop-how-to.md): Initiate drags, handle drops, provide visual feedback, and accept file drops in Avalonia.
- [How to: Work with Expander](/docs/how-to/expander-how-to.md): Learn how to use the Avalonia Expander control for collapsible content sections, accordion patterns, animated transitions, state binding, and custom headers.
- [How to: Work with Grid layouts](/docs/how-to/grid-how-to.md): Row and column definitions, sizing modes, spanning, shared sizing, and responsive Grid patterns.
- [How to: Display and Manipulate Images](/docs/how-to/image-how-to.md): Load images from various sources, handle aspect ratios, and manipulate bitmaps in Avalonia.
- [How to: Work with ItemsControl and ItemsRepeater](/docs/how-to/itemscontrol-how-to.md): Custom collection layouts with ItemsControl and ItemsRepeater beyond what ListBox offers.
- [How to: Work with ListBox](/docs/how-to/listbox-how-to.md): Selection handling, item templates, virtualization, styling, and advanced ListBox patterns.
- [How to: Work with Menus](/docs/how-to/menu-how-to.md): Learn how to use Menu, ContextMenu, and NativeMenu controls in Avalonia, including commands, keyboard shortcuts, dynamic items, checked items, submenus, and right-click context menus.
- [How to: Implement common MVVM patterns](/docs/how-to/mvvm-how-to.md): Learn how to implement common MVVM patterns in Avalonia using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm, including observable properties, commands, messaging, dependency injection, and validation.
- [How to: Navigate between views](/docs/how-to/navigation-how-to.md): Common patterns for switching between views and pages in Avalonia applications.
- [How to: Show notifications and toasts](/docs/how-to/notifications-how-to.md): Learn how to show overlay notifications, toast messages, status bars, and system tray notifications in your Avalonia application across desktop and mobile platforms.
- [How to: Build responsive layouts](/docs/how-to/responsive-layout-how-to.md): Create Avalonia layouts that adapt to different window sizes and form factors.
- [How to: Work with ScrollViewer](/docs/how-to/scrollviewer-how-to.md): Learn how to control scroll behavior, scroll programmatically, respond to scroll events, implement infinite scrolling, and handle nested scroll regions in Avalonia.
- [How to: Work with Slider](/docs/how-to/slider-how-to.md): Learn how to configure Slider ranges, display values, add tick marks, set orientation, and bind to view models in Avalonia UI.
- [How to: Style and theme controls](/docs/how-to/styling-controls-how-to.md): Customize control appearance with colors, variants, themes, and reusable styles in Avalonia.
- [How to: Work with TabControl](/docs/how-to/tabcontrol-how-to.md): Learn how to create static tabs, dynamic tabs, closeable tabs, and styled tabs using the Avalonia TabControl.
- [How to: Work with TextBox](/docs/how-to/textbox-how-to.md): Validation, formatting, input masking, selection, and TextBox customization in Avalonia.
- [How to: Switch between light and dark themes](/docs/how-to/theme-switching-how-to.md): Implement a light/dark theme toggle, persist the choice, and create theme-aware resources.
- [How to: Work with TreeView](/docs/how-to/treeview-how-to.md): Hierarchical data binding, lazy loading, selection handling, and customization with TreeView.
- [How to: Work with Windows](/docs/how-to/window-how-to.md): Sizing, positioning, dialogs, multi-window apps, startup behavior, and system chrome options.
- [Adding Interactivity](/docs/input-interaction/adding-interactivity.md): One of the fundamental things that a user interface must do is interact with the user. In Avalonia, you can add interactivity to your applications by leveraging events and commands. This guide will introduce events and commands with simple examples.
- [Commanding](/docs/input-interaction/commanding.md): Commanding connects user actions (button clicks, menu selections, keyboard shortcuts) to logic in your view model. Avalonia uses the standard .NET ICommand interface, which enables clean separation between UI and business logic.
- [Drag and Drop](/docs/input-interaction/drag-and-drop.md): Avalonia supports drag-and-drop operations for transferring data between controls or between your application and the operating system. The drag-and-drop system uses the DragDrop static class and the DataTransfer type to manage data during operations.
- [Focus](/docs/input-interaction/focus.md): Learn how to manage keyboard focus in Avalonia, including tab navigation, directional (XYFocus) navigation, focus events, pseudoclasses, and the FocusManager.
- [Gestures](/docs/input-interaction/gestures.md): Avalonia uses a unified pointer event system. Mouse, touch, and stylus input all flow through the same PointerPressed, PointerMoved, and PointerReleased events rather than having separate event types for each device. Pointer events tell you what the hardware did: a button went down, a finger moved.
- [Pinch](/docs/input-interaction/gestures/pinch-gesture-recognizer.md): A gesture recognizer that tracks a pinch gesture. A pinch gesture occurs when two pointer contacts are brought towards each other, or away from each other. This is useful in controls that implement a pinch-to-zoom interaction.
- [Pull](/docs/input-interaction/gestures/pull-gesture-recognizer.md): A gesture recognizer that tracks a pull gesture. A pull gesture occurs when a pointer is dragged from the edge of a control in a single, specific direction defined by the PullDirection property. The typical use case is pull-to-refresh, where the user drags down from the top of a list to trigger a data reload.
- [Scroll](/docs/input-interaction/gestures/scroll-gesture-recognizer.md): A gesture recognizer that tracks a scrolling gesture for panning content. ScrollGestureRecognizer detects when a pointer drags inside a control's bounds and translates the movement into scroll deltas, with support for inertia (the content continues scrolling after the pointer is released and gradually decelerates). It supports horizontal scrolling, vertical scrolling, or both simultaneously.
- [Keyboard and hotkeys](/docs/input-interaction/keyboard-and-hotkeys.md): Learn how to define hotkeys and key bindings in Avalonia using HotKey properties, KeyBindings, KeyGesture, and HotKeyManager for keyboard-driven commands.
- [Creating mouse and keyboard shortcuts](/docs/input-interaction/mouse-and-keyboard-shortcuts.md): Learn how to bind keyboard shortcuts and handle mouse events like double-click in Avalonia controls using KeyBindings, gestures, and context menus.
- [Pointer devices](/docs/input-interaction/pointer.md): Avalonia operates on an abstraction called 'pointer devices'. This can represent devices including, but not limited to, mouse, touchpad, and pen.
- [Routed events](/docs/input-interaction/routed-events.md): Most events in Avalonia are implemented as routed events. Routed events are events that are raised on the whole tree rather than just the control that raised the event.
- [Text Input and IME](/docs/input-interaction/text-input.md): Avalonia supports text input from keyboards, on-screen keyboards, and Input Method Editors (IMEs) for languages that require character composition, such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
- [Layout](/docs/layout.md): How the Avalonia layout system measures and arranges controls using panels and bounding boxes.
- [Choosing a layout panel](/docs/layout/choosing-a-layout-panel.md): Compare Avalonia panel controls and choose the right one for your layout scenario.
- [Positioning controls](/docs/layout/positioning-controls.md): Use HorizontalAlignment, VerticalAlignment, Margin, and Padding to position controls.
- [Responsive layouts](/docs/layout/responsive-layouts.md): Create layouts that adapt to different sizes using container queries, form-factor extensions, and reflowing panels.
- [.NET MAUI](/docs/migration/maui.md): Migrate from .NET MAUI to Avalonia or extend MAUI apps with the Avalonia MAUI Backend.
- [Other frameworks](/docs/migration/other.md): Migrate to Avalonia from Delphi, Qt, Electron, or ASP.NET MVC desktop applications.
- [Windows Forms](/docs/migration/winforms.md): Migrate Windows Forms applications to Avalonia for cross-platform support and modern UI.
- [WinUI / UWP](/docs/migration/winui.md): Migrate WinUI and UWP applications to Avalonia with equivalent XAML, controls, and MVVM patterns.
- [Migrating from WPF](/docs/migration/wpf.md): Migrate WPF applications to Avalonia with equivalent controls, bindings, and MVVM patterns.
- [WPF to Avalonia cheat sheet](/docs/migration/wpf/cheat-sheet.md): Quick reference mapping WPF concepts, controls, and APIs to their Avalonia equivalents.
- [Controls](/docs/migration/wpf/controls.md): Control hierarchy, naming, and behavioral differences between WPF and Avalonia controls.
- [Data templates](/docs/migration/wpf/data-templates.md): Differences between WPF and Avalonia data templates, type matching, and template storage.
- [Events](/docs/migration/wpf/events.md): Differences between WPF and Avalonia routed events, tunnelling, and event naming.
- [Layout](/docs/migration/wpf/layout.md): Layout system differences between WPF and Avalonia panels, sizing, and positioning.
- [Properties](/docs/migration/wpf/properties.md): Migrate WPF DependencyProperty usage to Avalonia StyledProperty and DirectProperty types.
- [Styling](/docs/migration/wpf/styling.md): Key differences between WPF and Avalonia styling, including selectors, style classes, and themes.
- [Developing with Avalonia for Android](/docs/platform-specific-guides/android.md): Setting up the Android development environment for building Avalonia applications, including SDK and workload installation.
- [Embedding Android native views](/docs/platform-specific-guides/android/embed-native-views.md): Learn how to embed native Android views such as WebView and Button controls inside your Avalonia application using NativeControlHost and AndroidViewControlHandle.
- [Register your app to open files](/docs/platform-specific-guides/android/intent-filter.md): Configure an Android intent filter so your Avalonia app can open files and custom URI schemes.
- [Platform-specific .NET](/docs/platform-specific-guides/dotnet.md): Overview
- [Embedded Linux](/docs/platform-specific-guides/embedded-linux.md): Avalonia supports running on embedded Linux devices without a desktop environment. This includes single-board computers like the Raspberry Pi, industrial panels, kiosks, and point-of-sale terminals. Instead of connecting through a windowing system like X11, Avalonia renders directly to the display hardware using either the Linux framebuffer or the Direct Rendering Manager (DRM).
- [Running Avalonia on a Raspberry Pi](/docs/platform-specific-guides/embedded-linux/raspberry-pi.md): Required hardware
- [Adding a virtual keyboard](/docs/platform-specific-guides/embedded-linux/virtual-keyboard.md): Accelerate
- [iOS](/docs/platform-specific-guides/ios.md): Setting up your developer environment
- [Desktop Linux](/docs/platform-specific-guides/linux.md): How Avalonia runs on desktop Linux, including WSL 2 setup and accessibility support with AT-SPI2.
- [macOS](/docs/platform-specific-guides/macos.md): How Avalonia runs on macOS
- [WebAssembly](/docs/platform-specific-guides/webassembly.md): Avalonia applications can run in the browser using WebAssembly (WASM). This page explains how to set up a project for browser deployment and how to use JavaScript interop.
- [Windows](/docs/platform-specific-guides/windows.md): Windows-specific Avalonia features including transparency, Mica, custom title bars, dark mode, DPI scaling, and Win32 interop.
- [Platform-specific XAML](/docs/platform-specific-guides/xaml.md): OnPlatform Markup Extension
- [Avalonia property system](/docs/properties.md): Avalonia has its own property system that extends the standard .NET property model. Avalonia properties provide support for styling, data binding, animation, property value inheritance, and change notification. Understanding the property system is essential for creating custom controls and working effectively with the framework.
- [Metadata and callbacks](/docs/properties/metadata-and-callbacks.md): Every Avalonia property has associated metadata that controls its default value, binding behavior, and optional coercion logic. You can specify metadata when registering a property and override it for derived types.
- [Property value inheritance](/docs/properties/property-value-inheritance.md): How Avalonia properties propagate values from parent to descendant elements in the visual tree, including built-in inherited properties and creating custom ones.
- [Property value precedence](/docs/properties/value-precedence.md): When multiple sources provide a value for the same Avalonia property (a local value, a style setter, an animation, an inherited value), Avalonia must decide which value wins. The property system resolves this using a fixed priority order defined by the BindingPriority enum.
- [Samples and tutorials](/docs/samples-tutorials.md): Explore Avalonia tutorials, sample apps, and quick guides to accelerate your learning.
- [Activatable lifetime](/docs/services/activatable-lifetime.md): API reference for IActivatableLifetime, the service that exposes application activation and deactivation events and background state methods.
- [Clipboard](/docs/services/clipboard.md): Data formats
- [File Dialogs](/docs/services/file-dialogs.md): Using the StorageProvider API to open file picker, save file, and folder picker dialogs in Avalonia applications.
- [Focus Manager](/docs/services/focus-manager.md): Manage keyboard focus in your Avalonia application using the FocusManager service to track, set, and clear the currently focused element.
- [Input Pane](/docs/services/input-pane.md): Monitor the platform's input pane (software keyboard) state, boundaries, and animation in Avalonia applications.
- [Insets Manager](/docs/services/insets-manager.md): Manage system bar visibility, safe area padding, and edge-to-edge display on mobile and browser platforms in Avalonia.
- [Launcher](/docs/services/launcher.md): Learn how to use the Avalonia Launcher service to open files, folders, and URIs in the user's default application.
- [Platform Settings](/docs/services/platform-settings.md): Access platform-specific settings such as tap sizes, double-tap timing, system colors, and hotkey configuration through the PlatformSettings service.
- [Bookmarks](/docs/services/storage/bookmarks.md): Bookmarks are particularly important for maintaining access to files and folders in modern operating systems that have strict security and privacy controls. For instance, on platforms like iOS and newer versions of macOS, direct file system access is heavily restricted. Instead, applications request the user to select a file or folder through a system-provided file picker, and the operating system then gives the application a security-scoped bookmark that it can use to access that file or folder in the future.
- [File Picker Options](/docs/services/storage/file-picker-options.md): Common picker options
- [Storage Items](/docs/services/storage/storage-item.md): Common members for StorageFile and StorageFolder
- [Storage Provider](/docs/services/storage/storage-provider.md): The StorageProvider is central to file and folder management. It provides methods for file and folder selection, checking platform capabilities, and interacting with stored bookmarks.
- [Container queries](/docs/styling/container-queries.md): Container queries allow styles to be activated for a control based on the size of an ancestor, which acts as a container.
- [Control template walkthrough](/docs/styling/control-template-walkthrough.md): This walkthrough builds a complete control template from scratch, explaining each component. By the end, you will understand how to re-template any Avalonia control.
- [Control themes](/docs/styling/control-themes.md): Control themes build upon styles to create switchable themes for controls. Unlike regular styles, which accumulate and cannot be removed once applied, a control theme can be replaced entirely. This makes control themes the right choice when you need to swap out a control's complete look for a specific instance or section of your UI.
- [Custom fonts](/docs/styling/custom-fonts.md): Avalonia supports custom TrueType (.ttf) and OpenType (.otf) fonts. You can embed font files in your application and reference them in XAML, removing any dependency on fonts installed on the host system.
- [Property setters](/docs/styling/property-setters.md): Property setters define what property values a style applies to a control after Avalonia has matched it using a selector.
- [Pseudoclasses](/docs/styling/pseudoclasses.md): Learn how to use pseudoclasses in Avalonia to apply styles based on control state such as focus, hover, or pressed.
- [Sharing styles](/docs/styling/sharing-styles.md): You can define styles in separate files and include them at any level of your application. This lets you share a consistent set of styles across multiple windows, user controls, or even across projects.
- [Styling best practices](/docs/styling/style-best-practices.md): This page collects practical guidelines for writing styles, themes, and templates in Avalonia. Following these patterns helps keep your styling code maintainable and performant.
- [Style classes](/docs/styling/style-classes.md): Learn how to assign and use style classes in Avalonia to apply conditional styling to controls.
- [Style precedence](/docs/styling/style-precedence.md): When multiple styles, local values, and animations target the same property on a control, Avalonia uses a fixed priority order to determine which value wins. Understanding this order is essential for diagnosing situations where a style does not appear to apply.
- [Style selector syntax](/docs/styling/style-selector-syntax.md): This page lists the XAML syntax for style selectors with the C# code methods that perform the same selection.
- [Style Selectors](/docs/styling/style-selectors.md): Learn how Avalonia style selectors match controls by type, class, name, and state using a CSS-like syntax.
- [Styles](/docs/styling/styles.md): Learn how to use Avalonia styles, selectors, and setters to share property settings between controls.
- [Setting theme variants](/docs/styling/theme-variants.md): Because theme variants are deeply integrated into the resource system, it is important to understand Avalonia resources.
- [Themes](/docs/styling/themes.md): In Avalonia, themes are complete sets of control themes and theme resources for built-in controls.
- [Typography](/docs/styling/typography.md): Font size, weight, style, stretch, letter spacing, line height, text decorations, and alignment properties for text.
- [Supported platforms](/docs/supported-platforms.md): Avalonia apps can target Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and WebAssembly. The level of support varies by operating system version, organised into three tiers. These tiers are reviewed with each major Avalonia release, so platforms may move between tiers over time.
- [Tags](/docs/tags.md)
- [3 docs tagged with "accelerate"](/docs/tags/accelerate.md)
- [One doc tagged with "xpf"](/docs/tags/xpf.md)
- [Testing](/docs/testing.md): Avalonia supports several testing strategies, each suited to different goals. You can combine them to build a layered test suite that covers view model logic, control behavior, visual output, and end-to-end user flows.
- [Headless Testing with NUnit](/docs/testing/headless-nunit.md): Set up and run headless UI tests for Avalonia applications using the NUnit test framework.
- [Headless Testing with XUnit](/docs/testing/headless-xunit.md): Set up and run headless UI tests for Avalonia applications using the XUnit test framework.
- [Headless Testing Platform](/docs/testing/setting-up-the-headless-platform.md): The headless platform runs Avalonia without a visible window, making it ideal for automated testing in CI/CD environments and on machines without a display. It provides the full Avalonia control tree, layout, styling, and data binding, but replaces the real windowing and rendering backends with in-memory implementations.
- [UI Testing with Appium](/docs/testing/ui-testing-with-appium.md): Appium is an open-source automation framework that drives your application through its accessibility tree, simulating real user interactions such as clicking buttons, typing text, and verifying control states. Unlike headless tests, which run without a visible window and simulate input programmatically, Appium tests launch your compiled application in a real window and interact with it the same way a user would.
- [Welcome](/docs/welcome.md): Documentation for Avalonia, the cross-platform .NET UI framework. Build apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and WebAssembly from a single codebase.
- [XAML reference](/docs/xaml.md): This section provides a reference for the XAML language features available in Avalonia. While the Avalonia XAML fundamentals page covers basic concepts, this reference goes deeper into syntax, directives, markup extensions, and the XAML compilation pipeline.
- [XAML compilation](/docs/xaml/compilation.md): Avalonia uses the XamlX compiler to process .axaml files at build time. Unlike WPF (which interprets XAML at runtime by default), Avalonia compiles XAML into IL code during the build, providing faster startup, smaller binaries, and compile-time error detection.
- [x: directives](/docs/xaml/directives.md): XAML directives are special attributes in the x: namespace that control how the XAML engine processes elements. They are part of the XAML language specification, not specific to any particular control.
- [Generic types in XAML](/docs/xaml/generics.md): Use generic .NET types in Avalonia XAML with the x:TypeArguments directive for collections and custom controls.
- [Markup extensions](/docs/xaml/markup-extensions.md): Markup extensions are special expressions enclosed in curly braces {} that provide dynamic values in XAML. They extend what you can express beyond simple string literals.
- [XAML namespaces](/docs/xaml/namespaces.md): XAML namespaces tell the XAML engine where to find the types referenced in your markup. Every Avalonia XAML file requires at least one namespace declaration to work.
- [Type converters](/docs/xaml/type-converters.md): How XAML type converters transform string attribute values into .NET types, including built-in converters and creating custom converters.

## Tools

- [Avalonia Tools](/tools.md): Professional developer tools for Avalonia. Debug visually, package effortlessly, and build faster with Avalonia Accelerate.
- [AI Tools](/tools/ai-tools.md): Avalonia provides MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that connect AI coding assistants to documentation, running applications, and packaging workflows. Rather than copying error messages or describing your UI in text, your AI assistant can search the official docs, inspect the visual tree, take screenshots, set properties, and package your app for you.
- [Build MCP](/tools/ai-tools/build-mcp.md): Set up the Build MCP server so your AI coding assistant can search Avalonia guides, tutorials, API references, and get step-by-step migration guidance.
- [Community edition](/tools/community-edition.md): The Accelerate Community Edition provides free access to professional development tools for individual developers, small organizations, and educational institutions. The aim of this license is to allow the majority of Avalonia developers to benefit from significantly improved tooling at no cost while ensuring the sustainable development of the Avalonia ecosystem.
- [Assets tool](/tools/developer-tools/assets-tool.md): Use the Developer Tools Assets panel to browse, search, preview, and copy URIs for all embedded Avalonia resources in your running application.
- [Attaching applications](/tools/developer-tools/attaching-applications.md): Accelerate
- [Attaching DevTools to the previewer](/tools/developer-tools/attaching-to-the-previewer.md): Learn how to attach Avalonia Developer Tools to a XAML previewer process for visual tree inspection and diagnostics.
- [Attaching DevTools to the remote tool](/tools/developer-tools/attaching-to-the-remote-tool.md): Accelerate
- [Breakpoints tool](/tools/developer-tools/breakpoints-tool.md): The Application Breakpoints Tool allows you to monitor and debug property changes and events in Avalonia applications without modifying code. Breakpoints can be set on properties, events to help diagnose issues and understand application behavior.
- [Elements tool](/tools/developer-tools/elements-tool.md): The Elements Tree presents a unified view that combines both visual and logical hierarchies. It loads only visible elements to optimize performance, while organizing the structure with the logical tree as the foundation. Template contents are collapsed away within /template/ node contents.
- [Events tool](/tools/developer-tools/events-tool.md): The Events Tool provides real-time monitoring and debugging capabilities for Avalonia's routed event system. Routed events in Avalonia follow a sophisticated event handling mechanism where events can traverse up or down the visual tree. This tool helps developers track event propagation, identify event handlers, and debug event-related issues in their applications.
- [Installing the Accelerate developer tools](/tools/developer-tools/installation.md): Accelerate
- [Logs tool](/tools/developer-tools/logs-tool.md): View and filter Avalonia log messages in Developer Tools, integrate with Microsoft.Extensions.Logging, and create custom log sources such as Serilog sinks.
- [DevTools MCP](/tools/developer-tools/mcp.md): Set up the DevTools MCP server to let AI assistants inspect, debug, and modify your running Avalonia application.
- [Metrics tool](/tools/developer-tools/metrics-tool.md): Monitor application health using counters, histograms, and other .NET metric instruments in Avalonia Developer Tools, and create custom metric sources.
- [Developer tools options](/tools/developer-tools/options.md): DeveloperToolsOptions.Gesture
- [Application profiler tool](/tools/developer-tools/profiler-tool.md): While metrics primarily contain a single dimension of information, suitable for displaying as a chart, profilers capture richer data. After a recording is completed, Developer Tools aggregates the results and displays them as a table.
- [Resources tool](/tools/developer-tools/resources-tool.md): Inspect, navigate, and edit your Avalonia application's resource hierarchy at runtime using the Resources tool in DevTools.
- [Developer tools settings](/tools/developer-tools/settings.md): Settings page can be accessed from the Tray Icon menu (on Windows and Linux) or the macOS global menu.
- [Developer Tools Shortcuts](/tools/developer-tools/shortcuts.md): Keyboard shortcut reference for Avalonia Developer Tools, covering inspection, search, navigation, layout, and tool-switching commands.
- [FAQ](/tools/faq.md): General
- [IDE Support](/tools/ide.md): Avalonia works with the .NET IDEs you already use. Whether you prefer Visual Studio, VS Code, or Rider, you can build Avalonia applications with full IntelliSense, debugging, and project support out of the box. Where things differ is the XAML editing experience, specifically previewing, code completion, and designer tooling.
- [Installing Avalonia Accelerate](/tools/installing-accelerate.md): Configure your project to use Avalonia Accelerate NuGet packages and add your license key.
- [Using the legacy developer tools](/tools/legacy-developer-tools.md): This documentation covers the Legacy Developer Tools. A new AvaloniaUI Developer Tools is now available, offering enhanced features and improved debugging capabilities. The Legacy Developer Tools remain supported with stability updates.
- [Parcel command line reference](/tools/parcel/command-line-reference.md): Parcel provides a command-line tool that packages Avalonia apps for Windows, macOS, and Linux. It includes app signing and packaging capabilities to simplify distribution of ready-to-install binaries.
- [Parcel MCP](/tools/parcel/mcp.md): What is Parcel MCP?
- [Packaging apps for Linux](/tools/parcel/packaging-for-linux.md): Package Avalonia applications for Linux using Parcel, with support for DEB, RPM, ZIP, and AppImage formats, including dependency management and desktop integration.
- [Packaging apps for macOS](/tools/parcel/packaging-for-macos.md): Accelerate
- [Packaging apps for Windows](/tools/parcel/packaging-for-windows.md): Accelerate
- [Setting up Avalonia Parcel](/tools/parcel/setup.md): Install, configure, and activate Avalonia Parcel, the packaging tool for building, signing, and packaging Avalonia applications on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
- [Tags](/tools/tags.md)
- [10 docs tagged with "accelerate"](/tools/tags/accelerate.md)
- [2 docs tagged with "ai"](/tools/tags/ai.md)
- [2 docs tagged with "mcp"](/tools/tags/mcp.md)
- [Configure Android debugging on Linux](/tools/visual-studio-code/configure-vscode-debug-linux.md): Set up Visual Studio Code on Linux to build, deploy, and debug Avalonia Android projects using the Mono debugger.
- [Avalonia for Visual Studio extension](/tools/visual-studio-extension.md): Use the Avalonia Visual Studio extension for enhanced XAML editing with IntelliSense, error highlighting, and a built-in UI previewer.

## Troubleshooting

- [Troubleshooting](/troubleshooting.md): Find guidance on diagnosing and resolving common issues in Avalonia apps, including installation, performance, platform-specific behaviour, and UI development.
- [App performance issues](/troubleshooting/app-performance-issues.md): Is your app running too slowly? The performance of Avalonia applications can be significantly improved by taking into account several key considerations during the development process. This document discusses the steps you can take to optimize the performance of your Avalonia applications.
- [MediaPlayer issues](/troubleshooting/controls/mediaplayer.md): Troubleshoot common MediaPlayer problems in Avalonia, including playback failures, black screens, memory leaks, and platform-specific codec issues.
- [MessageBox](/troubleshooting/controls/messagebox.md): Learn why Avalonia does not include a built-in MessageBox and how to add message box functionality using third-party libraries.
- [Installation troubleshooting](/troubleshooting/installation.md): Solutions for common issues when installing the .NET SDK, Avalonia templates, and NuGet package sources.
- [macOS issues](/troubleshooting/platform-specific-issues/macos.md): App menu
- [WebAssembly issues](/troubleshooting/platform-specific-issues/webassembly.md): Troubleshoot common issues when running Avalonia applications in the browser via WebAssembly, including native library errors, build failures, and performance considerations.
- [Windows issues](/troubleshooting/platform-specific-issues/windows.md): Troubleshoot Windows-specific Avalonia issues including signing, rendering, scaling, and dark theme title bar problems.
- [Tags](/troubleshooting/tags.md)
- [2 docs tagged with "accelerate"](/troubleshooting/tags/accelerate.md)
- [Developer tools issues](/troubleshooting/tools/developer-tools.md): Troubleshoot common Developer Tools problems including connection failures, missing logs, and diagnostic configuration.
- [Styles](/troubleshooting/ui-development/styles.md): Selector has no targets
- [Themes](/troubleshooting/ui-development/themes.md): Troubleshooting common issues with Avalonia UI themes, including missing control themes, unintended style overrides, and transparent application windows.

## Xpf

- [Avalonia XPF](/xpf.md): Avalonia XPF runs your existing WPF applications on macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, and WebAssembly with minimal code changes.
- [Centralizing multiple XPF projects](/xpf/configuration/centralizing-multiple-xpf-projects.md): Learn how to centralize XPF SDK version management and license key configuration across multiple projects in a single repository.
- [Customizing initialization](/xpf/configuration/customizing-initialization.md): How to access and customize the Avalonia AppBuilder API in an XPF application by disabling automatic initialization.
- [Performance Optimization](/xpf/configuration/performance.md): Reducing startup time with ReadyToRun
- [Linux Deployment](/xpf/deployment/linux.md): How to publish and deploy XPF applications on Linux, including required native libraries and runtime dependencies.
- [macOS Deployment](/xpf/deployment/macos.md): How to publish and deploy XPF applications on macOS, including app bundle structure and code signing.
- [Windows Deployment](/xpf/deployment/windows.md): Learn how to publish, package, and deploy your Avalonia XPF application on Windows, including self-contained builds, single-file publishing, and installer options.
- [Frequently Asked Questions](/xpf/faq.md): .NET version compatibility
- [Getting started](/xpf/getting-started.md): If you use an AI coding assistant that supports MCP (VS Code, Cursor, Rider, Claude Code, and others), the Build MCP server can walk you through this process step by step. Ask your assistant to migrate your WPF project and it will analyze your dependencies, configure the NuGet feed, switch the SDK, and troubleshoot issues interactively. See Build MCP setup for configuration instructions.
- [Embedding Avalonia in XPF](/xpf/interop/embedding-avalonia-in-xpf.md): Embedding Avalonia controls
- [Getting native window handles](/xpf/interop/native-window-handles.md): How to retrieve native platform window handles in XPF applications, including the difference between virtual and native handles.
- [Using XPF in Avalonia](/xpf/interop/using-xpf-in-avalonia.md): Host WPF-compatible XPF controls inside an existing Avalonia application using XpfContainer.
- [Web Content Embedding](/xpf/interop/web-content.md): XPF provides several options for embedding web content in your application. The right choice depends on your target platforms and embedding requirements.
- [Clipboard](/xpf/migration/clipboard.md): Learn how the WPF Clipboard API works in XPF across Windows, macOS, and Linux, including text, bitmap, and custom data format support.
- [Mapping keys](/xpf/migration/key-mapping.md): How to remap keyboard shortcuts in XPF applications so that platform-specific key combinations feel native on macOS and Linux.
- [Known Differences from WPF](/xpf/migration/known-differences.md): Overview
- [Removing the titlebar](/xpf/migration/removing-the-titlebar.md): By default, XPF applications include a titlebar for window management. However, there may be cases where you want to remove the titlebar. This guide walks you through the process of removing the titlebar.
- [Linux](/xpf/platforms/linux.md): Supported distributions
- [macOS](/xpf/platforms/macos.md): Application name
- [Mobile and Browser](/xpf/platforms/mobile-and-browser.md): Prerequisites
- [Windows](/xpf/platforms/windows.md): Windows-specific considerations when running XPF applications, including Win32 API behavior and compatibility notes.
- [Headless Testing](/xpf/testing/headless-testing.md): Overview
- [Library Compatibility](/xpf/third-party/compatibility.md): This page covers compatibility notes for specific third-party libraries with Avalonia XPF. For enabling Win32 API shims (required by most third-party libraries on non-Windows platforms), see Win32 API Shims.
- [Win32 API Shims](/xpf/third-party/win32-api-shims.md): Avalonia XPF implements WPF's API surface, however a variety of third-party libraries also depend on various Win32 APIs which are not available cross-platform.
- [WinAPI Shim Reference](/xpf/third-party/winapi-reference.md): This page lists the Win32 APIs available through XPF's shim layer. These shims exist to support third-party WPF controls on non-Windows platforms. They are not a general-purpose Win32 emulation layer.
- [Troubleshooting](/xpf/troubleshooting.md): Trouble restoring NuGet packages
- [Missing features](/xpf/version-info/missing-features.md): A summary of WPF features that are unavailable, limited, or planned for future support in Avalonia XPF.
- [Release notes](/xpf/version-info/release-notes.md): XPF 1.6.2 (2026-03-20)
- [XPF versioning](/xpf/version-info/versioning.md): Learn how to select and configure XPF package versions, including stable releases and nightly builds.
